name: Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W
description: Ultra-compact, low-power RPi with quad-core 64-bit Cortex-A53 in the original Pi Zero footprint

status.comment: Raspberry Pi obsolescence statement commits to production until at least January 2030.

architecture-family.comment: Quad-core 64-bit Cortex-A53; can also run 32-bit (ARMv7) userspace.

open-source-hardware.comment: Raspberry Pi publishes mechanical drawings and partial schematics but not full PCB design files under an OSHW-compatible license.

country-of-origin.comment: Designed by Raspberry Pi Ltd (Cambridge, UK). Boards manufactured in the UK and elsewhere.

soc.comment: RP3A0 is a Raspberry Pi-branded system-in-package combining the Broadcom BCM2710A1 die (same as the launch RPi 3) with 512 MB LPDDR2.

soc-vendor.comment: Silicon die is Broadcom BCM2710A1; the RP3A0 SiP packaging is by Raspberry Pi.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Stock clock 1.0 GHz; slightly down-clocked from the original RPi 3's 1.2 GHz to fit Zero thermal envelope.

process-node.comment: BCM2710A1 die fabricated on 40 nm (same die as the original RPi 3 launch SoC).

vulkan-support.comment: VideoCore IV has no official Vulkan driver. A non-conformant community driver (RPi-VK-Driver) exists but is not vendor-supported.

hw-video-decode.comment: Vendor lists H.264 and MPEG-4 decode at 1080p30. MPEG-4 is not in the Lineup tag set; only H.264 captured.

hw-video-encode.comment: H.264 encode at 1080p30 per vendor specs.

ram-type.comment: Vendor describes RAM as 512 MB LPDDR2 SDRAM in the SiP. Lineup tag set has no LPDDR2 option; mapped to closest available 'lpddr3' tag — actual technology is LPDDR2. Tag gap worth filling.

ethernet.comment: No Ethernet port; networking is Wi-Fi/Bluetooth only.

usb-a-2-ports.comment: No full-size USB-A. Has one micro-USB OTG data port and one micro-USB power-only port.

max-display-resolution.comment: Vendor specs cap display output at 1080p60 over HDMI.

mipi-dsi.comment: Pi Zero 2 W has CSI-2 (camera) but no DSI (display) connector.

mipi-csi.comment: Mini 22-pin CSI-2 camera connector (same physical type as RPi Zero W).

audio-jack.comment: No 3.5 mm jack; audio is over HDMI or via solder pads.

gpio-pins.comment: 40-pin HAT-compatible header footprint shipped unpopulated; user solders the header.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Pin-compatible 40-pin footprint, but Pi Zero form factor (65×30 mm) does not match the standard HAT mechanical envelope (RPi-standard 85×56 mm). pHATs designed for the Zero footprint do fit.

power-input.comment: Micro-USB power input; vendor-recommended supply is 5V 2.5A.

idle-power-w.comment: CNX-Software measured ~120 mA @ 5V (~0.6 W) idle on Raspberry Pi OS Bullseye Lite. Can be tuned down to ~0.375 W with HDMI/Wi-Fi disabled. Date checked: 2026-04-26.

load-power-w.comment: CNX-Software measured ~635 mA @ 5V (~3.2 W) under sbc-bench full-load on four cores at 1.2 GHz. Stress combinations with peripheral activity peaked higher (~6.75 W). Date checked: 2026-04-26.

cooling-required.comment: No vendor cooling requirement; thermal mass of the SiP is small. A heatsink helps under sustained four-core load but is not required by the vendor.

weight-g.comment: Reseller and community measurements report ~9–10 g; vendor product page does not publish a weight value.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Stock upstream kernel boots with VC4 graphics, USB, Wi-Fi (Cypress CYW43436) and Bluetooth working; Arch Linux ARM and Raspberry Pi OS both ship working kernels.

official-os-image.comment: Raspberry Pi OS (Bookworm) is published and maintained by the vendor.

distros-with-images.comment: Ubuntu Server has documented Zero 2 W support since 21.10 (32-bit; 64-bit needs minor config). Armbian is NOT included — Armbian does not officially build for Raspberry Pi boards.

bootloader.comment: Boots via the Broadcom VideoCore firmware (proprietary), then loads Linux. U-Boot can be chained but is not the primary boot.

long-term-support.comment: Raspberry Pi obsolescence statement commits to production until at least January 2030 — 4+ years remain from 2026 check date.

android-support.comment: No official Android image. Community LineageOS / KonstaKANG builds exist for some RPi boards but are not maintained for the Zero 2 W.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Launch MSRP $15. Vendor product page now lists $15. Date checked: 2026-04-26.

available-regions.comment: Sold via Raspberry Pi's global Approved Reseller network.

typical-lead-time.comment: In stock at major resellers (Pi Hut, Adafruit, Pimoroni) as of 2026-04-26 — supply normalized after the 2022–2023 shortage.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive vendor documentation portal: getting-started, hardware reference, datasheets, configuration guides.

community-size.comment: Raspberry Pi has the largest hobbyist Linux SBC community by a wide margin.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Wide ecosystem of cases, official camera modules, pHATs and Zero-form-factor add-ons. Note: full-size HATs do not fit the Zero footprint.
